bugs.description: issueCertificate supersedes an existing issued certificate during a normal issue call instead of rejecting unless the regenerate path explicitly opts in.

notes: Automated vitest passed. The required Playwright certificate contract command is blocked before product assertions by Clerk auth setup redirect loops; a diagnostic --no-deps run then fails because certificate fixture env vars such as EVENT_A_ID are absent. Typecheck fails with broad pre-existing repo errors outside the certificate issuance module. Manual code/spec review found a production behavior gap, but fixing it safely would require reconciling existing non-contract tests that currently assert the conflicting supersession behavior.

bugs.description: After a transaction failure retry was consumed, issueCertificate could throw the retry error or continue into an unintended third attempt instead of re-throwing the original transaction failure.

notes: Full Vitest passed after the fix (231 files, 4736 tests), including focused certificate issuance unit and mutation-kill suites (126 tests). Playwright certificate contracts remain blocked before product assertions by Clerk auth setup redirecting /events with ERR_TOO_MANY_REDIRECTS. Repo-level typecheck still fails on broad existing errors outside this certificate issuance change. Manual verification confirmed the transaction retry state now stops after one retry and preserves the original transaction error on retry failure; persistence was not re-probed because the available contract browser fixture is blocked.

bugs.description: activateCertificateTemplate always issued the archive-sibling update even when no active sibling template existed, violating the no-sibling path that should only activate the target template.

notes: Full Vitest passed after the fix (231 files, 4736 tests), including focused certificate action and full-journey suites (114 tests). Playwright certificate contracts remain blocked before product assertions by Clerk auth setup redirecting /events with ERR_TOO_MANY_REDIRECTS. Repo-level typecheck still fails on broad existing errors outside this certificate activation change, and a targeted typecheck grep found no certificate action errors. Manual review confirmed eventId-scoped target lookup, eventId-scoped sibling lookup, transactional archive-before-activate ordering when a sibling exists, and the corrected no-sibling activation-only path. Persistence was not re-probed because the available contract browser fixture is blocked.

Full Vitest passed (231 files, 4736 tests), and focused certificate issuance plus issue/regenerate route suites passed (4 files, 151 tests). Playwright certificate contracts remain blocked before product assertions by Clerk auth setup redirecting /events with ERR_TOO_MANY_REDIRECTS. Repo-level typecheck still fails on broad existing errors outside this story; targeted typecheck output showed only a certificate-issuance test-file regex target error, with no production certificate issuance or route errors. Manual verification confirmed issueCertificate returns the inserted id, certificateNumber, and verificationToken; production issue/regenerate route consumers map those fields into their response bodies; the UI server-action caller ignores the return value. Persistence was not re-probed through the browser contract because the authenticated fixture is blocked.

Full Vitest passed (231 files, 4736 tests). Playwright certificate contracts remain blocked before product assertions by Clerk auth setup redirecting /events with ERR_TOO_MANY_REDIRECTS. Repo-level typecheck still fails on broad existing errors outside this story. Independent Stryker dry-run with the locked stryker.config.json succeeded and ran 4578 tests without executing mutants. Manual verification accepted outcome b: .quality/state.json and qa/baselines/state-baseline-20260415T070514Z.json contain 72 scored modules, the committed snapshot has a plain-English partial-baseline note explaining the 30 unscored files and exit 143 timeout, no stryker.conf.mjs shadow config exists, and the travel-flow baselines are preserved at travel.ts=99.48, travel-cascade.ts=97.03, validations/travel.ts=100.

bugs.description: Archived events reached the certificate API auth catch-all and returned 403 instead of the contract-required 400 event archived response.
The single-issue certificate API ignored submitted body eventId mismatches instead of returning 400 and capturing the tenancy mismatch in Sentry.

notes: Full Vitest passed after the fix (231 files, 4736 tests), and the focused single-issue certificate route suite passed (15 tests). Playwright certificate contracts remain blocked before product assertions by Clerk auth setup redirecting /events with ERR_TOO_MANY_REDIRECTS. Repo-level typecheck still fails on broad existing errors outside this story; the visible output remains in QA/test, Sentry, people, navigation, notification, and cascade files rather than the certificate API route. Manual review confirmed the route derives eventId from the URL, rejects body eventId/event_id mismatches via assertEventIdMatch with Sentry capture, maps archived-event auth failures to 400, keeps ops/read_only at 403, returns cross-event access through the sanitized 404 path, validates body and CME variables with Zod, and returns the inserted certificate id, certificate_number, verification_token, and issued status. Persistence was not re-probed through the browser contract because the authenticated fixture is blocked.

bugs.description: issueCertificate persisted caller-supplied renderedVariablesJson verbatim, allowing stale or empty master-person fields to become the immutable rendered_variables_json snapshot.

notes: Full Vitest passed after the fix (231 files, 4736 tests), and focused certificate issuance plus issue/regenerate route suites passed (4 files, 151 tests). Playwright certificate contracts remain blocked before product assertions by Clerk auth setup redirecting /events with ERR_TOO_MANY_REDIRECTS. Repo-level typecheck still fails on broad existing errors outside this story; a targeted post-fix typecheck grep found no errors in certificate production paths. Manual review confirmed templateSnapshotJson is copied from the active template at insert time, renderedVariablesJson is now merged from authoritative people master fields before insert, and issuedCertificates update paths only touch status, supersession, revocation, download, verification, and sent-at fields, not templateSnapshotJson or renderedVariablesJson. Persistence was not re-probed through the browser contract because the authenticated fixture is blocked.

bugs.description: Archived events reached the regenerate API auth catch-all and returned 403 instead of the contract-required 400 event archived response.
The regenerate API ignored submitted body eventId/event_id mismatches instead of returning 400 and capturing the tenancy mismatch in Sentry.

notes: Full Vitest passed after the fix (231 files, 4736 tests), and focused regenerate route plus certificate issuance suites passed (3 files, 136 tests). Playwright certificate contracts remain blocked before product assertions by Clerk auth setup redirecting /events with ERR_TOO_MANY_REDIRECTS. Repo-level typecheck still fails on broad existing errors outside this story; a targeted typecheck grep found no errors in the regenerate production route or certificate-issuance production file. Manual review confirmed the route derives eventId and certificateId from validated URL params, validates the otherwise-empty body with Zod, rejects body eventId/event_id mismatches via assertEventIdMatch with Sentry capture, maps archived-event auth failures to 400, keeps ops/read_only at 403, event-scopes old certificate and active-template lookups, delegates insertion and same-transaction supersession update to issueCertificate, and returns 201 with the new certificate id, new certificate_number, and old supersedes_id. Persistence was not re-probed through the browser contract because the authenticated fixture is blocked.

bugs.description: Archived events reached the revoke API auth catch-all and returned 403 instead of the contract-required 400 event archived response.
The revoke API ignored submitted body eventId/event_id mismatches instead of returning 400 and capturing the tenancy mismatch in Sentry.

notes: Full Vitest passed after the fix (231 files, 4736 tests), and the focused revoke route suite passed (10 tests). Playwright certificate contracts remain blocked before product assertions by Clerk auth setup redirecting /events with ERR_TOO_MANY_REDIRECTS. Repo-level typecheck still fails on broad existing errors outside this story; a targeted grep of the typecheck output found no revoke-route, event-id-mismatch, or event-access errors. Manual review confirmed the route derives eventId and certificateId from validated URL params, trims and requires a non-empty reason with Zod, rejects body eventId/event_id mismatches via assertEventIdMatch with Sentry capture, maps archived-event auth failures to 400, keeps ops/read_only at 403, event-scopes select/update queries with withEventScope, rejects already-revoked and non-issued certificates before update so the original revoked_at remains unchanged, leaves superseded_by_id untouched, and returns 200 with id, status, and revoked_at. Persistence and public verify were not re-probed through the browser contract because the authenticated fixture is blocked; schema inspection found issued_certificates has revoked_at and revoke_reason but no revoked_by column, so no schema change was made under the no-schema-change rule.

bugs.description: The bulk worker never released the acquired Redis lock after a queued run completed, so successful or failed runs could block retries until TTL expiry.
The bulk API enqueued certificateType/scope/lockKey data that the bulk worker type and recipient/template loader did not understand, allowing a 202 response to queue a doomed generation run.
The bulk certificate API ignored submitted body eventId/event_id mismatches instead of returning 400 and capturing the tenancy mismatch in Sentry.
Archived events could reach the bulk API auth catch-all and return 403 instead of the contract-required event archived response.

notes: Full Vitest passed after the fix (231 files, 4736 tests), and focused bulk route plus Inngest bulk worker suites passed (2 files, 23 tests). Playwright certificate contracts remain blocked before product assertions by Clerk auth setup redirecting /events with ERR_TOO_MANY_REDIRECTS. Repo-level typecheck still fails on broad existing errors, but a targeted grep of the post-fix typecheck output found no errors in the touched bulk route, bulk worker, or Inngest event type files. Manual review confirmed SET NX EX 300 acquisition, 409 lock-holder metadata, lock deletion by the worker finally block on success/failure, API payload compatibility for certificateType/scope runs, archived-event 400 mapping, role 403 behavior, and body eventId/event_id mismatch rejection through assertEventIdMatch. Persistence was not re-probed through the browser contract because the authenticated fixture is blocked.

bugs.description: Bulk generation returned a batch_id but never persisted or exposed the per-batch success/error summary required for failure review and retry decisions.
Already-issued certificates in a resume run were counted as successful before their PDF refresh/update completed, so a refresh failure could inflate the bulk summary.

notes: Full Vitest passed after the fix (231 files, 4736 tests), and focused bulk worker plus bulk route suites passed (2 files, 23 tests). Playwright certificate contracts remain blocked before product assertions by Clerk auth setup redirecting /events with ERR_TOO_MANY_REDIRECTS. Repo-level typecheck still fails on broad existing errors outside this story; a targeted grep of post-fix typecheck output found no errors in the touched bulk route, bulk worker, or bulk generation state helper. Manual review confirmed recipients and issued-certificate queries remain event-scoped, each new certificate insert/supersession update is inside a per-cert transaction, per-recipient exceptions are caught and logged while later recipients continue, issued_certificates has only issued/superseded/revoked terminal statuses with new rows defaulting to issued, existing current certificates are reused without duplicate inserts, and the worker now stores completed/failed batch summaries in Redis for GET /api/events/[eventId]/certificates/bulk?batch_id=... with auth, role, event, and batch_id validation.

Full Vitest passed (231 files, 4736 tests), and focused CME route/validation suites passed (3 files, 61 tests). An independent schema probe confirmed valid complete CME variables and credit_hours equal to event duration are accepted, while credit_hours above duration, zero, negative, string-valued, missing fields, and blank string fields are rejected. Playwright certificate contracts remain blocked before product assertions by Clerk auth setup redirecting /events with ERR_TOO_MANY_REDIRECTS. Repo-level typecheck still fails on broad existing errors outside this story. Manual review confirmed the route derives event duration from the URL eventId row, runs createCmeVariablesSchema before issueCertificate, returns flattened Zod field errors with 400 for invalid CME variables, and issueCertificate persists renderedVariablesJson from a snapshot that starts with caller variables before adding authoritative person fields. Direct DB persistence was not re-probed because the authenticated contract fixture is blocked.

bugs.description: The implementation exposed only /api/verify/[token], so the contract-required public /verify/[token] JSON route would 404.
lookupAndVerify returned success before verification_count and last_verified_at persistence completed, allowing the required counter update to race or fail silently.
The superseded certificate number lookup was scoped only by certificate id and skipped the verified certificate eventId.

notes: Full Vitest passed after the fix (231 files, 4736 tests), including focused public verify route/serializer suites (11 tests). Certificate Playwright contracts remain blocked before product assertions by Clerk auth setup redirecting /events with ERR_TOO_MANY_REDIRECTS. Repo-level typecheck still fails on broad existing errors outside this story; visible failures remain in QA/test, Sentry, people, tab-bar, notification, and cascade files rather than the public verify production paths. Manual verification confirmed /verify/[token] now has a public JSON route, invalid tokens return 400 JSON, response serialization emits only allowed public fields, revoked and superseded optional fields are conditionally emitted, verification_count/last_verified_at are updated with an awaited atomic increment, and superseded number lookup is event-scoped. Live DB re-query was not performed because the authenticated contract fixture is blocked and no seeded public verification token was available in this loop.

Full Vitest passed (231 files, 4736 tests), and the focused download route suite passed (10 tests). Certificate Playwright contracts remain blocked before product assertions by Clerk auth setup redirecting /events with ERR_TOO_MANY_REDIRECTS. Repo-level typecheck still fails on broad existing errors outside this story; a targeted grep of the typecheck output found no errors in the certificate download route or immediate production dependencies. Manual verification confirmed params are Zod-validated before processing, assertEventAccess gates the event, the certificate lookup is event-scoped with withEventScope, missing and cross-event certificates return 404, revoked certificates return 404 for coordinator/ops/read_only while super_admin receives a signed URL, issued and superseded certificates receive signed URLs, the R2 provider helper is called with the private storageKey and 300-second TTL, and the issued_certificates schema stores a private storage_key with no permanent public download URL column.
